hard.next_step: Confidence 30% 2nd Jul - sent intro email for Jihad and Hakim, to follow up with meeting 10th June - RFP response submitted, some discount retained for future negotiation. 3rd June - Clarifications received - will rebuild the commercials accordingly, there is some complexity around phasing of the business units, but will bundle into one (as we did with SAMI). 25th May - RFP deadline extended to 10th June. 7th May - RFP received, under review - clarifications deadline 15th May, Response deadline 20th May. 30th Mar - Re below - to help him build out budget approval I have built a quick ROI calculator and shared with him a draft output, suggesting we build it out together and co-present to the CFO - may help move it forward and get further up the decision ladder. 25th Mar - Update from CPO: discussions regarding the bid management solution are still underway as we work on securing budget approval, so some activities are on hold for now; moving back to Q2. 13th Mar - nudged to arrange a catch up after Eid. 23rd Jan - CPO update: procurement strategy submitted to management for review and approval; selection process to follow approval. DQ: want to try and turn Jihad into a champion - offered live demo f2f in Abu Dhabi, business case support, regular cadence. 7th Jan - Pricing submitted to CPO. 30th Oct - Met Jihad (CPO) and Arun onsite in Abu Dhabi, pricing template run-through; gaps in data for the QIT; shortlisting of Ariba and Coupa looks informal; est 3-6 months. 9th Sept - CPO attended Abu Dhabi World Tour event - running a formal evaluation, shortlisted Ariba and Coupa, interested in adding Zycus.
hard.ais_why: On 23 Jan 2026 CPO Jihad framed the initiative as digitalization and automation of procurement, adjacent to AI but not naming AI/Merlin/agentic. RFP received 7 May 2026 and Jihad is engaging deeply, but the conversation is digitalization/ROI focused; ANA is the only AI SKU in scope and there is no explicit AI demand from the buyer, so the score sits at 5 (boundary down per rubric). Confidence Medium.

forecast_read.recommended_forecast: Pipeline with close date moved to Q4 2026 (Oct-Dec)
forecast_read.reason: The deal is genuinely alive (RFP response submitted 10 Jun 2026, buyer reply 3 Jul 2026), but 22 days to a 31 Jul 2026 close cannot fit RFP evaluation feedback, CFO budget approval, vendor selection and contracting. This is a date problem on an engaged deal, not a dead deal.
forecast_read.math: 22 days to close as of 2026-07-09. Formal Evaluation entered 7 May 2026 (63 days in stage). Close date already slipped twice (16 Feb 2026: 30 May to 30 Jun; 25 Mar 2026: 30 Jun to 31 Jul). RFP submitted 10 Jun 2026 with no evaluation feedback in 29 days; budget approval has been in progress since 23 Jan 2026 with no confirmed green light. Remaining steps (evaluation outcome, selection, budget sign-off, contracting) cannot compress into 22 days at this account's observed pace.

metrics.narrative: The stated business drivers are group-wide efficiency and total cost of ownership reduction: on the 8 Oct 2025 call the buyer said the group wants to 'achieve efficiency of our work, reduce the total cost of ownership somehow' and to 'benefit from the AI tools that integrated with the system', but no dollar figure or cycle-time metric has ever been quantified. Zycus tried to close that gap on 30 Mar 2026 by building a draft ROI calculator and proposing to co-present it to the CFO, and re-nudged on 13 Apr 2026, but there is no confirmation the session happened or that a number was agreed. Quantifying savings with Jihad remains the open move.
sources: SF task 13 Apr 2026 (ROI Analysis follow-up email)

economic_buyer.narrative: Jihad Ibrahim (Group Director of Procurement) is the confirmed Decision Maker but explicitly does not hold budget authority: his 25 Mar 2026 email says discussions are on hold 'as we work on securing budget approval'. The rep's 30 Mar 2026 plan to build an ROI calculator and 'co-present to the CFO' names the CFO as the approval seat, but no CFO has appeared on any call or email in the evidence read, and no budget approval has been confirmed since. The RFP issuance on 7 May 2026 implies some internal approval progressed, but the commercial approver remains unengaged by Zycus.
sources: SF contact roles (no CFO engaged on the opportunity)

decision_criteria.narrative: On the 8 Oct 2025 call the buyer laid out explicit criteria: group-wide applicability across the holding's companies, efficiency and TCO reduction, AI-enabled tooling, implementation approach (in-house vs a Big Four partner such as EY), and module/user-based pricing structure. These were formalized when the buyer issued 'RFP/100206/NH/2026: Group-Wide Source-to-Pay (S2P) Platform Implementation' on 7 May 2026, and refined in the 3 Jun 2026 clarification round where business-unit phasing complexity was resolved by bundling into one commercial structure.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a formal multi-vendor RFP: shortlisted Coupa and SAP Ariba before adding Zycus (CPO's own account, 9 Sep 2025), RFP issued 7 May 2026, clarifications answered 19-22 May 2026, deadline extended to 10 Jun 2026, and Zycus submitted on 10 Jun 2026 per the buyer's bid@nh.ae instructions. What follows submission is internal management review then vendor selection, per the CPO's 23 Jan 2026 note that selection follows management approval of the procurement strategy. No evaluation timeline or award date has been communicated 29 days after submission, which is the process's biggest unknown.
sources: SF tasks 7 May - 11 Jun 2026 (RFP correspondence with Arun Joy)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics have not started (consistent with Formal Evaluation), but the procurement paper trail has begun: the buyer required a signed NDA before releasing clarifications (Arun Joy, 22 May 2026) and Zycus completed vendor registration on 29 May 2026, sending the signed COC acknowledgement, signed confidentiality agreement (with a mutuality clause added) and vendor master documents. MSA, SOW and InfoSec have not been discussed and will only surface after a selection decision.
sources: SF task 22 May 2026 (Arun Joy email: share the signed NDA)
SF task 29 May 2026 (New Vendor Registration documents sent)

identify_pain.narrative: The CPO articulated the pain on the 8 Oct 2025 call: fragmented, manual procurement across a multi-entity group, with the goal of 'efficiency of our work' and reducing total cost of ownership, tied to a budget-cycle decision window. His 23 Jan 2026 email confirmed the strategic driver in his own words: 'a primary focus on the digitalization and automation of our procurement function', with the strategy submitted to management for approval. The pain owner is Jihad Ibrahim; the impact is not yet quantified.

champion.narrative: Jihad Ibrahim is a developing champion, not a proven one. The prior owner explicitly set out on 23 Jan 2026 'to turn Jihad into a champion', offering a live f2f demo and business-case support. What has developed: Jihad engages directly and transparently (23 Jan and 25 Mar 2026 status emails, RFP invitation to Zycus), and he replied within a day to the 2 Jul 2026 ownership-transition email even while on leave (3 Jul 2026). What is missing: he needs repeated nudging (13 Mar, 25 Mar 2026), has not brokered access to the CFO, and the relationship must now transfer from Dan Quinn to the new RVP Mohamad Alhakim (owner change 29 Jun 2026).
sources: SF task 3 Jul 2026 (Jihad reply while on leave)

competition.narrative: National Holding shortlisted Coupa and SAP Ariba before Zycus was added, per the CPO's account at the 9 Sep 2025 Abu Dhabi World Tour event, and all three are presumed in the same RFP that closed 10 Jun 2026. On the 8 Oct 2025 call the buyer asked directly 'if you are comparing Coupa and SAP Ariba, your product will be closer to which one?' and whether all their features are available in Zycus; the rep positioned Zycus closer to Coupa with an AI-maturity wedge (generative AI studio since 2018). No evaluation feedback since submission, so relative standing is unknown.

critical_signals.text: The full RFP response went in on 10 Jun 2026 with some discount deliberately held back for negotiation, and 29 days later the buyer has shared no evaluation feedback or award timeline.

Jihad Ibrahim replied on 3 Jul 2026 that he is on leave and will pick up the introduction to the new regional lead Mohamad Alhakim when he returns, so the relationship is live but paused on his return.
This is a live three-way evaluation against Coupa and SAP Ariba, both shortlisted by the buyer before Zycus was invited in, with no signal yet on who leads.
The 31 Jul 2026 close date cannot fit evaluation feedback, CFO budget approval, selection and contracting; budget approval has been pending since 23 Jan 2026.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is genuinely in the fight at National Holding: invited into a formal group-wide S2P RFP by an engaged Group Director of Procurement, response submitted on the 10 Jun 2026 deadline with discount retained for negotiation. But it is a three-way contest against Coupa and SAP Ariba with no buyer-voiced preference, no CFO engagement, budget approval unconfirmed since January, and a close date 22 days out that the process cannot meet.

win_position.text: Zycus was proactively added by CPO Jihad Ibrahim to an existing Coupa/SAP Ariba shortlist (9 Sep 2025) and carried through to a formal group-wide S2P RFP, with the response submitted on deadline 10 Jun 2026.

Pricing is fully worked: commercials were rebuilt after the 3 Jun 2026 clarifications to bundle the business units into one structure, and some discount was retained for future negotiation per the 10 Jun 2026 note.

Jihad Ibrahim engages directly and transparently (status emails 23 Jan and 25 Mar 2026, reply within a day on 3 Jul 2026 even while on leave), and vendor registration and NDA were completed in May 2026.
No buyer-voiced preference for Zycus exists anywhere in the evidence; the last substantive competitive signal is the buyer's own 8 Oct 2025 question about feature parity with Coupa and SAP Ariba.
The CFO who must approve budget has never been engaged; the 30 Mar 2026 ROI calculator built to co-present to the CFO was never confirmed used, and budget approval has been pending since 23 Jan 2026.
The deal is single-threaded through Jihad Ibrahim, who is now on leave during a rep transition from Dan Quinn to Mohamad Alhakim (owner change 29 Jun 2026).

deal_momentum.text: The buyer engaged through the whole RFP round: clarifications answered 19-22 May 2026, deadline extension granted to 10 Jun 2026, and submission instructions issued by Arun Joy on 11 Jun 2026.
Jihad Ibrahim replied on 3 Jul 2026 to the new-RVP introduction, confirming the thread is live and he will re-engage after his leave.
Since the 10 Jun 2026 submission there is no buyer-communicated evaluation timeline, no scheduled meeting, and no dated next milestone; all July activity is rep-side transition email.
The buyer has never initiated contact unprompted in 2026; every touch followed a Zycus nudge, and the close date has already slipped twice (16 Feb and 25 Mar 2026).

customer_commitment.text: The buyer invested real process effort in Zycus: issuing the RFP to Zycus (7 May 2026), running a clarification round, extending the deadline, and requiring NDA and vendor registration (22-29 May 2026).

Commitment is procedural, not preferential: no demo has been taken since Oct 2025 despite repeated offers (19 Jan 2026 live Abu Dhabi demo offer went untaken), and the ROI co-build offer of 30 Mar 2026 got no confirmed uptake.

deal_risk.text: Close date 31 Jul 2026 is 22 days out with evaluation, budget approval, selection and contracting still ahead; the honest close is Q4 2026.
Budget approval has been 'in progress' since 23 Jan 2026 with activities explicitly 'on hold' on 25 Mar 2026 and no confirmed green light since.
Single-thread through Jihad Ibrahim during his leave and a rep handover; the named Executive Sponsor Chad Bautista (Procurement Officer) has never appeared in any call or email read.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer-voiced preference for Zycus exists. The strongest signal is structural and stale: the CPO proactively asked to add Zycus to an existing Coupa/Ariba shortlist (9 Sep 2025) and said 'we are interested to move on to the next step' on 8 Oct 2025. In the last 60 days the buyer has run Zycus through the RFP process neutrally, with identical process treatment implied for all bidders.

business_case.evidence: Efficiency and TCO reduction stated by the CPO (8 Oct 2025) and digitalization/automation strategy confirmed in his 23 Jan 2026 email, but never quantified. The 30 Mar 2026 draft ROI calculator Zycus shared to co-present to the CFO was never confirmed built out or presented.

deal_movement.summary: The deal moved from Qualified to Formal Evaluation on 7 May 2026 when the buyer issued its group-wide S2P RFP; Zycus submitted on 10 Jun 2026 and the amount was raised to 342,667 the next day (then 345,000 on 30 Jun 2026). Ownership transferred from Dan Quinn to new RVP Mohamad Alhakim on 29 Jun 2026 and the introduction to the buyer landed 2-3 Jul 2026.

items.change: Buyer contact Jihad Ibrahim acknowledged the new-RVP introduction, noting he is on leave and will respond on return
Introduction emails for new owner Mohamad Alhakim sent to Jihad Ibrahim by Carl Kimball and Mohamad
Owner changed from Dan Quinn to Mohamad Alhakim
RFP response submitted to bid@nh.ae per buyer instructions; amount raised 288,584 to 342,667 off the rebuilt commercials
Stage advanced Qualified to Formal Evaluation on receipt of RFP/100206/NH/2026
Close date pushed 30 Jun 2026 to 31 Jul 2026 (second push; first was 16 Feb 2026, 30 May to 30 Jun)

day_summary.overall: The last day with real deal activity was 3 Jul 2026, when Jihad Ibrahim replied to the leadership-transition emails: he thanked Carl Kimball for introducing Mohamad Alhakim as the new regional lead, said he is currently on leave, and committed to review and respond once he resumes work. Carl replied warmly, proposing lunch or an office visit when he is in region in the coming weeks. The deal itself did not move; the RFP submitted 10 Jun 2026 remains in evaluation.

items.name: Jihad acknowledges new RVP introduction
items.summary: Jihad Ibrahim, on leave, confirmed he received the introduction to Mohamad Alhakim and will review and respond when back at work

items.name: Carl proposes in-person connect
items.summary: Carl Kimball replied offering lunch or an office meeting with Jihad and Mohamad when in region in the next few weeks

competitive_position.summary: A live three-way formal evaluation. Coupa is the strongest current threat: the buyer's own framing on 8 Oct 2025 asked how Zycus compares to Coupa and SAP Ariba, and Zycus positioned itself 'closer to Coupa', making Coupa the closest functional rival in the buyer's mental model. Both Coupa and SAP Ariba were shortlisted by the buyer before Zycus was invited in (9 Sep 2025) and both are presumed to have bid in the same RFP that closed 10 Jun 2026. No evaluation feedback has surfaced, so no rival is evidenced as ahead.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Like, if you are comparing Coupa and SAP Ariba, your product will be closer to which one?
competitors.source: Avoma call 8 Oct 2025 (buyer question); Next_Step__c 9 Sep 2025 (shortlisted)
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with the AI-maturity gap (generative AI studio since 2018, positioned as years ahead) and value for money, since Zycus itself frames Coupa as the closest functional comparison; press Coupa's weaker CLM and sourcing named on the 8 Oct 2025 call.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Zycus shortlisted alongside Coupa and SAP Ariba following the CPO's evaluation at the Abu Dhabi World Tour event.
competitors.how_we_win: Position modern AI and UX against an under-invested legacy platform; no buyer objection to Ariba is recorded, so the differentiation must be proven in the RFP evaluation and any orals, not assumed.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: The buyer's stated criteria are group-wide S2P applicability, efficiency and TCO reduction, AI-enabled capability, flexible implementation approach, and workable module/user pricing. Zycus is aligned or partially aligned on all of them, with pricing the one axis where the buyer has pushed hardest and where the final read awaits RFP evaluation.

best_practice_check.summary: The deal is in a formal RFP evaluation with no visibility into timeline or standing, single-threaded through a Decision Maker on leave, mid rep-transition, and carrying an indefensible close date. The levers are evaluation visibility, CFO-level business case, and multi-threading through the new RVP's f2f presence in Abu Dhabi.
flags: Get evaluation visibility: use Arun Joy (Senior Procurement Officer, the RFP process owner) to confirm the evaluation timeline, orals/demo round and award date, so the deal is not dark inside the buyer's own process.
Take the business case to power: revive the 30 Mar 2026 ROI calculator as a co-built CFO presentation with Jihad; budget approval, pending since 23 Jan 2026, is the deal's structural blocker.

De-risk the single thread and the handover: Mohamad Alhakim should convert Carl's 3 Jul lunch offer into an in-person Abu Dhabi meeting with Jihad on his return, and pull Arun Joy and the wider evaluation team into the relationship.

expansion_context.note: No sibling Closed-Won evidence on this account in the data read; this is net-new business.

scope_change.detail: Commercials were rebuilt after the 3 Jun 2026 RFP clarifications to bundle the group's business units into one structure (as done with SAMI), lifting the amount to 342,667 on 11 Jun 2026 and 345,000 on 30 Jun 2026. Product scope is a broad upstream-plus suite: iContract, iSource, iSupplier, iRequest, iRisk and ANA.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus led its competitive story with AI maturity (generative AI studio since 2018, claimed 7-8 years ahead) on the 8 Oct 2025 call and the buyer responded to AI-integrated tooling as a benefit, but the buyer's own framing is 'digitalization and automation' rather than AI, and ANA is the only AI SKU in scope. The AI wedge is asserted, not yet anchored in the buyer's evaluation criteria.

ai_fit_signal.summary: The buyer volunteered interest in benefiting 'from the AI tools that integrated with the system' (8 Oct 2025) and their RFP scores AI capability implicitly through a digitalization lens, but no explicit AI demand or Merlin/agentic language appears in buyer communications.

confidence_signals.summary: The deal reads live, not cooling: the buyer ran Zycus through the full RFP round in May-June 2026 (clarifications, extension, submission instructions) and Jihad replied within a day on 3 Jul 2026 despite being on leave. The caution is that all engagement is buyer-process or reply-to-nudge; the buyer has not initiated unprompted in 2026 and post-submission silence on evaluation is unexplained.

The 30 Oct 2025 onsite meeting in Abu Dhabi has no recording or Avoma summary; its content is known only from the Next_Step__c note.
Speaker labels in the 8 Oct 2025 transcript are numeric; buyer speakers attributed by role, not name.

✅ Jihad Ibrahim replied on 3 Jul 2026 to the new-RVP introduction, keeping the thread two-way even while on leave.

waiting_on_buyer.item: Confirmation of CFO/management budget approval for the procurement digitalization program
waiting_on_buyer.source: Jihad email 25 Mar 2026: activities 'on hold' while 'securing budget approval'; no confirmed green light since

best_practices.item: Confirm the RFP evaluation calendar (scoring, orals/demo round, award date) with Arun Joy so the deal is not blind inside the buyer's process
Co-build the ROI business case with Jihad and secure a joint session with the group CFO to unlock the budget-approval blocker
Multi-thread the account: engage Chad Bautista and the wider evaluation team via a tailored use-case demo (the buyer asked on 8 Oct 2025 for a physical demo 'engaging more team members')

sum.headline: No change in the last 24h; the deal last moved on 3 Jul when Jihad Ibrahim, currently on leave, acknowledged the introduction to new regional lead Mohamad Alhakim and committed to respond on his return.
